title: Sev1 Incident Command Orchestration
description: An incident-and-change response workflow that coordinates mitigation, approvals, and execution sequencing under operational urgency. Focus case: sev1 incident command orchestration.
assumptions: Incident lifecycle is managed as a single authoritative case (T1) and all actions refer to that case.
Approval tokens and reservation tokens are mutually tracked and are required before execution steps.
Runbooks and rollback plans exist for change items targeted for execution.
Execution windows are available and can be allocated deterministically prior to implementation.
risks: Delayed approvals or unavailable approvers can block mitigation and extend outage.
Resource reservation collisions may lead to conflicting changes if tokens are not correctly enforced.
Incomplete runbook validation increases likelihood of failed executions and need for rollback.
Insufficient stakeholder coordination can cause unapproved changes or missed compliance requirements.
